task: Maintain and review the enterprise OSHA 300/300A/301 injury and illness log across all branches.
frequency: as incidents occur, with weekly review and annual ITA submission
tool_today: OSHA forms, incident reports, HRIS, ITA portal
decision_content: Decide whether an incident is recordable, whether it triggers 8-hour or 24-hour OSHA reporting, and whether a branch classification is defensible.

task: Monitor DOT driver qualification files and hours-of-service exceptions across the CDL delivery fleet.
frequency: daily ELD exception review plus quarterly qualification-file audit
tool_today: ELD system, FMCSA portal, driver qualification files, compliance spreadsheet
decision_content: Judge whether a driver qualifies for short-haul exceptions, whether an expiry or HOS event requires intervention, and when a branch issue becomes a fleet-wide risk.

task: Administer the DOT drug and alcohol testing program for CDL drivers, including random-pool coverage and post-accident follow-up.
frequency: as hires, random selections, and qualifying incidents occur
tool_today: Consortium/MRO portal, FMCSA rules, employee files, notification workflow
decision_content: Decide whether an event triggers required testing, whether the program is meeting minimum coverage, and when a driver can return to duty.

task: Track operator certification and training currency for forklifts, cranes, and other regulated equipment classes across all branches.
frequency: ongoing expiry monitoring plus pre-deployment checks for regulated equipment
tool_today: LMS, certification tracker, HRIS, training vendor portal
decision_content: Determine which certifications apply to each equipment class, whether a credential is current and acceptable, and when a lapsed operator must be pulled from work.

task: Run cross-branch safety audits and track corrective-action closure before gaps become repeat findings.
frequency: quarterly audits with monthly corrective-action follow-up
tool_today: Audit checklists, digital inspection platform, action tracker, branch follow-up
decision_content: Assess finding severity, decide which items need immediate escalation, and determine whether a pattern is local drift or a company-wide procedural problem.

task: Investigate workplace incidents and near-misses and manage time-sensitive OSHA reportable-event response.
tool_today: Incident investigation forms, OSHA reporting portal, equipment history, branch escalation calls
decision_content: Decide whether an event is reportable, what the likely root cause is, whether equipment should be taken out of service, and what corrective action is required.

task: Manage the enterprise safety training program, including completion tracking, recertification reminders, and toolbox-talk distribution.
frequency: monthly training follow-up with annual recertification waves and new-hire triggers
tool_today: LMS, training calendar, toolbox-talk library, email, branch self-reporting
decision_content: Prioritize which training gaps are regulatory versus recommended, which branches need intervention first, and when a gap is severe enough to restrict duties.

task: Assemble the enterprise safety KPI pack for operations and leadership review from incidents, audits, training, and driver-behavior signals.
frequency: monthly operating review and quarterly leadership rollup
tool_today: OSHA log exports, audit tracker, LMS reports, ELD reports, spreadsheet or BI dashboard
decision_content: Choose which trends require escalation and which branches or categories deserve added audit attention.
